entry.title: Re: Welcome

entry.title: Re: About Myself
entry.content: It's great to have you here @Vinoth_Ari !! Let us know your area of expertise or which SAP topics you are most interested in?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to share a link to a library without S...
entry.content:
Emelie, 

I'll make the assumption that the external user in this case has SAP IAS access. If yes, then when you generate the Direct Link, set the "Learning Only Users" to Yes (refer attached screenshot). 

 

In the event the user does NOT yet have an SAP IAS account, you can use "Sites" functionality. Let me know if you need to go into that. 

Thank you, 

Sumanth

entry.title: Re: after create a new banner, the old title in the text...
entry.content: Tamiri, the old title is probably going to autocorrect in 24 hours. If it doesn't, please post a screenshot. Sorry it's network cache and we are mostly LMS experts here, and our suggestions/advice is at best based on our past experiences. Rest assured (based on our experience) your issue should resolve itself in the next 24 hours.

entry.title: Re: Final settlement still credit customer after accrual...
entry.content:
What you describe is standard behavior. The settlement amount can be equal, larger or smaller than the accrual. Condition type REU1 is setting the accrual amount to zero, but it is not influencing the settlement amount.

Within the standard customizing scales are used for setting the target / rebate threshold. If done right this ensures that a settlement amount of zero is calculated if the target / rebate threshold is not met. 

Based on your description I assume that in your case somebody decides manually if the target / rebate threshold is met. Setting REU1 will not impact the settlement amount. Instead you need a 100% discount condition that is part of the pricing procedures for accrual and settlement.

entry.title: can't open this link - replicate SQLA to msSQL
entry.content:
I can also provide some further technical information about synchronizing to Microsoft SQL Server:

  • The general tutorial for working with MobiLink in Sybase Central and with a MobiLink Synchronization Model is here:

    http://dcx.sap.com/index.html#sa160/en/mlstart/sqlatut.html

    Initially, you may want to follow it "as-is" (which uses a SQL Anywhere consolidated database) in order to get a better understanding of the MobiLink product. You can then move on to using a Microsoft SQL Server consolidated database afterwards.

  • Changing the tutorial to work with a different consolidated database is mostly related to creating an ODBC DSN that targets a Microsoft SQL Server instance. You would pick that DSN when defining a consolidated database in your MobiLink synchronization model. The rest of the instructions regarding creating scripts and models is the same, but using Microsoft SQL Server SQL syntax (T-SQL) instead of SQL Anywhere syntax inside the synchronization scripts.

  • You will need to install the MobiLink system tables for Microsoft SQL Server. The script to do this can be found at: %SQLANY16%\MobiLink\setup\syncmss.sql. If you are using Sybase Central to create a Synchronization Model though, once you connect to Microsoft SQL Server, you will be prompted to install the MobiLink system tables automatically (i.e. run the script).

  • There are some Microsoft SQL Server specific issues that you will want to be aware of:
    http://dcx.sap.com/index.html#sa160/en/mlserver/ml-basics-mss-5159304.html

I have contacted some people internally here at SAP about consulting engagements to see if they can assist. If you are already working with an SAP Sales representative, it would probably be best to inquire with them directly and look into your available options.

entry.title: Re: MessageTransformBean module parameter for header line in SFTP Receiver adapter
SAp Enable Now Producer Connection error

I have re downloaded the App launcher, first repair, then - removed and re installed again,  Still same message 

Any ideas ? 

Thank you

entry.title: CPI - if function returns multiple values
entry.content:
the if function is returning always the true value and the false value together in the same context, which makes my target structure take the false value (as being the first value).

I could manage the issue with the following udf-script and mapping:

def void conditionalMappingSingle(String[] conditions, String[] trueValues, String[] falseValues, Output output, MappingContext context) {
    // Loop through the conditions and find the first true value
    for (int i = 0; i < conditions.length; i++) {
        if ("true".equalsIgnoreCase(conditions[i])) {
            output.addValue(trueValues[i]); // Add the corresponding true value to the output
            return; // Exit after finding the first true value
        }
    }
    // If no true value is found, add the first false value to the output
    if (falseValues.length > 0) {
        output.addValue(falseValues[0]); // Default to the first false value
    }
}

Using if (standard function)

 

 

using user-defined function:

 

Isn't there a way to solve it with the standard mapping functions?

entry.title: Executive Review 'look & feel'
entry.content:
Hello

I am looking for some advice please regarding the look of the Executive Review display screen.

When viewing Executive Review, primarily on a laptop screen, there is hardly any space for the rows of user data. You can usually only see 3 or 4 rows. I know there is an expand arrow on the top right, but this doesn't really do anything (expands to just 5 or 6 rows).

Just wondered if others are experiencing this too, and if anyone has any tips on how best to view the Executive Review screen (without having to massively zoom out!).

Thanks, Sam

SAP Build contains all those other products, but SAP Build Code is just an IDE (BAS) with the addition of Joule and some guidelines. Is that correct?

entry.title: Re: GRIR unable to clear due to Reference Key 3 not diffe...
entry.content: try to untick special treatment account GR/IR in selection screen

entry.title: Re: MessageTransformBean module parameter for header line...
entry.content:
To add header line with all field names you can simply add one field with sample name like "datafields" and map the field with constant. In constant you can give all the field names along with static field separately.

datafields -> mapped with constant -> field1,field2,field3 ( for comma separated field separator)

 

This works in my case as the first field will be represented as all field names followed by your actual fields

entry.title: Merge two cubes based on two common references?
entry.content: I want to start with my orders database and merge another query related to shipments. Sometimes the common field between the two queries is one reference and sometimes it is another. It is possible to create a cube that pulls the shipment data based on the match from the first reference, then shipment data based on the match from the second reference if the first is null or no match?

Hi All,

What are the integrations or touch points in OTC flow if we are using S4 HANA without using EWM but only TM.

- First touch point is when we create a Sales order , here we get freight unit from TM.

- Any other touch points from TM once planning is completed for example once Freight order is created?

entry.title: Sold subscription product on invoice issued from S4hana ublic cloud
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Since last year, the ID and description of the subscription product has been removed from the invoice form, so now only the ID and description of the rate element is printed.

When I look at the field mapping of the standard integration flow for the Transfer of Billing Data to Sales Billing (https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/integration/transfer-of-billing-data-to-sales-billing) I see that info "SoldProduct" is mapped and transferred to the billing document request.

Do you know if it can be found in any CDS view?
For example, BillingPerformancePeriodStrDte and BillingPerformancePeriodEndDte are also not part of the standard output form template but they can be accessed via CDS view I_BillingDocumentItemBasic but here the rate element ID is considered the "product number".

Thank you in advance for your input.

entry.title: Re: Goods issue in S/4 without using EWM for delivery note printout
entry.content: Why would it be printed before goods issue?

entry.title: Reverse a Material to dekit the components
entry.content:
Thank You in Advance

Looking for a tcode / process on how to take a material where the production order is fully closed and want to dekit the component material back into inventory.

Example  Material A qty 10

Component 1, Component 2, Component 3 = 1 each per assembly.

Transaction take Material A inventory removed / reduced to 0, Component 1,2,3 inventory created for 10 each.

entry.title: BPMON qRFC monitoring alert rating is showing as gray even though health is Green.
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have configured qRFC monitoring for one of the production system to know the stuck queues more than 30min. this was working fine earlier but now the rating for this alert is showing as gray even though the health is green and also BPMON data collection is not happening. This is a RFC pull metric. Can you please suggest how to troubleshoot this data collection or gray alert issue in BPMON.

entry.title: Re: Still cannot install Crystal Reports 2016
entry.content:
I can't find that KBA now so no idea what it was suggesting but from other post with the KBA it possibly could be due to missing Windows Framework version.

And to answer your question, you can have both CRD and CR for VS installed on the same PC.

Very strange that CRforVS will install but not CRD??????

FYI CR 2016 is end of life now... so if you have CR 2020 use it.

Try going here and getting CR 2016 SP 9:

https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

File you are looking for is SAP Crystal Reports 2016 SP09 - ONE

It should work on either Windows 10 or 11.

It's definitely environmental now since you get the same error on both OS's and really suggests your AV/Firewall settings but... Or you don't have full Admin rights, ask one of the IT guys to install it using their log on info to see what they get, that will rule out a permission issue.

Go to Programs and features and copy the SAP... installs, hold ALT key and the hit Print Screen and you can then paste it into Paint and and copy just the SAP installs, it might be something else causing this.

And do the same for Windows Framework installs, this is mine, I have Windows 11, VS 2022 and CR 2020 installed: Note C++ 2013 is required.

When installing I believe there is a folder called \2 in here:

C:\Users\YourUserName\AppData\Local\Temp

While CR is installing it creates this temp folder to log in the install info, once the install is complete it copies it over and deletes the temp log.

See if the install log file exists and look in it to see if it catches the error.

entry.content:
Hi,

 

it would be always really good, if you add the information about which release you talk about, are you on the latest Private Cloud/OnPrem release, are you on Public Cloud?

 

But the answer is: There is no such functionality.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: Replicate same source object with different target na...

entry.title: SAP Vendors with same VAT number (UK VAT group)
entry.content:
Hello team,

Please see below a query from one of my customers. Any recommendation/feedback is much appreciated!

Apparently, when a group of companies registers as a VAT Group, the UK tax office assigns them a VAT number, so the members of that VAT group have the same VAT number (in our case, they are 8-9 vendors).

Therefore, there are 8-9 vendors that have the same VAT number, but they are different vendors (each engaged in a particular activity).

As SAP by default handles duplicity of VAT number when generating the vendors, it would give us an error when creating these vendors. Is there any UK specific functionality that covers this? or what would be the standard SAP solution?

Kind regards,

Blanca

entry.title: Re: Please advise how to write on WBS Budget Availability...
entry.content: Hello Susanne, thank you for getting back, good to know. Based on this information we should consider the original scenarios (which i paste below). We looked for a Standard API to leverage for this but could not find one. Is that something that can be confirmed? Alternatively how would SAC be able to recall an custom API? or would option 1 be the most advisable solution? Thank you very much for the support. If Standard API is not available which of the below solution is advisable? 1. Write first from SAC to ACDOCP leveraging SAC standard export to S/4HANA through SAC Data Management and then move the data to BPJA e BPGE in S/4 2. Recall a custom API from SAC and leverage an existing FM (RFC KBPP_EXTERN_UPDATE_CO) to write on BPJA e BPGE in S/4.

entry.title: Restrict CR types based on Authorization Group in MDG-BP
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I have a requirement in which i have to restrict CR types based on Authorization groups

For ex - If a CR type is for Hungary then when doing the change from edit button in UI only HU change requrest should be visible rest others should be deleted.

I am trying to create an implicit enhancement in get_data method of cl_mdg_bs_cr_types_feeder class to put a condition like

If data model EQ 'BP'.

then will check for authorization and based on that i will implement a conditional statement to delete unwanted CR's but in the above method i debugged a lot but i'm not able to find the field in which authorization group is stored.

Can someone help me out?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit : Migrate your Data using Staging Tables a CO-Cost Center Object c
entry.content:
Great post, @bassem_chteoui!

Just one small note about the "Prerequisite" section (right before "Step 1"):

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the following business role is required:

  • SAP_BR_CONFIG_EXPERT_DATA_MIG

You create and assign the required business role for data migration using the business role template and adjust it to the user's requirements.

As you can check above, this role alone indeed suffices the authorization prerequisites only when SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is being used!

If an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise or Cloud Private Edition is being used, an additional role will be required:

To transfer data using the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, a user with the role SAP_CA_DMC_MC_USER is required in the SAP S/4HANA system. This user must also have the role SAP_BR_CONFIG_EXPERT_DATA_MIG for the front-end server.

Sources:

  • Data Migration - Migrate Data Using Staging Tables | SAP Help Portal
  • Data Migration - Roles and Authorizations | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,
Caio

entry.title: Unable to make the payment form as empty for the certain condition type under the lease contracts
entry.content:
Unable to make the payment form as empty for the certain condition type under the lease contracts, especially after the migration of such lease contracts.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to make the payment form as empty for the cert...
entry.content:
For example if the condition type is maintained as Exercise Price, the payment form maintained with any inputs (eg: In advance) which should be empty.

But it is not editable, In such scenarios you can give the add row under the conditions tab, once you give the add row, the payment form field will be empty for the exercise price and you can save it. This will also avoid some error messages after the migration of lease contracts.

entry.title: Re: Order Release error in preventive maintenance
entry.content:
Check the date of creating TASK list and compare with Order Basic Start date.

Should the same or Order start date greater than task list created date.

entry.title: Sap Datasphere Catalog
entry.content:
I am building some data assets in the development environment and have been configuring the data catalog, terms, glossary. However, after setting up the initial terms, I tried to test how this could be exported to the productive environment or tenant in DataSphere, but I cannot find any option. Is there a way to migrate terms, KPIs, glossaries, and tag hierarchies from the development environment to the productive environment?, Is it necessary to configure both environments separately?,  

When an object is included in a package and migrated to the production environment, does the migration include the tags associated with it in the data catalog?

I couldn't find any documentation about that, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Sending data in excel file from SAP to a shared folder of Virtual machine
entry.content: What have you tried thus far and what problems are you running into?

entry.title: Re: Creating extrinsic fields for ExpectedUnplanned/MaximunUnplanned using Ariba CIG

entry.title: Re: Creating extrinsic fields for ExpectedUnplanned/Maxim...
entry.content: This is the tag referred to that needs to be updated

entry.title: Problem when calling a method from an include program
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a problem with a method call that has me, to say the least, perplexed.

The method is very simple. It receives a single parameter (table name) and returns a date:

  METHOD ultima_fecha_pbi.
    "--------------------------------------------------------------------*
    "  CLASS-METHODS GET_ULTIMA_FECHA_PBI
    "  IMPORTING
    "     !TABLA TYPE ANY
    "     returning
    "     VALUE(ULTIMA_FECHA) TYPE LASDA .
    "--------------------------------------------------------------------*
    SELECT SINGLE ultima_fecha FROM zlog_tablas_pbi
      WHERE tabla = @tabla  "<------ Table name
      INTO @ultima_fecha.   "<------ Date
  ENDMETHOD.

When called from a simple test program (no includes), the method works perfectly, i.e., returns the data as expected.

CONSTANTS c_tabla_sap TYPE string VALUE 'ZLISFACT'.  "<-- Table name
DATA g_ultima_fecha   TYPE dats.                     "<-- date

PERFORM buscar_ultima_fecha USING    c_tabla_sap
                            CHANGING g_ultima_fecha.
"-------------------------------------------------------------------
FORM buscar_ultima_fecha USING    nombre_tabla   TYPE any
                         CHANGING p_ultima_fecha TYPE dats.
  p_ultima_fecha = zcl_pbi=>ultima_fecha_pbi( tabla = nombre_tabla ).
ENDFORM.

If I check the data just after the SELECT (using debug) I get:

  TABLA = ZLISFACT (Table name - Data type CString{8})
  HEX = 5A004C00490053004600410043005400

So far, so good but, if I call exactly the same method with the same parameters (and using exactly the same FORM) from a report program with includes, the SELECT returns SY-SUBRC 4, even though the data (seen using debug) appears to be the same:

TABLA =  ZLISFACT
HEX = 5A004C00490053004600410043005400

Any ideas?

 

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: SEND NOTIFICATIOS/ ALERTS BETWEEN USERS IN S4 HANA CL...
entry.content:
Dear Ismael,

Thank you for using our community to submit your question.

To address your request about the possibility of SAP S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition allowing users to communicate, we suggest using SAP Collaboration Manager.

SAP Collaboration Manager is an integrated chat solution within the SAP Fiori launchpad that enables efficient task completion in business applications. Some key features include:

- Creating chats with colleagues to collaborate on activities
- Taking notes and sharing documents, images, and video links
- Capturing and annotating screenshots directly within the application
- Etc..

This tool enhances user efficiency by providing seamless collaboration features without requiring system switches. It allows for instant communication and task initiation, improving overall productivity and team coordination.

Below are some links about SAP Collaboration Manager and how to integrate it with S/4Hana Cloud:

User Help for SAP Collaboration Manager 

Integrating SAP Collaboration Manager with S/4Hana Cloud 

I hope this information can help you.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Get the Best Out of Your SAP CX Solutions with CQC Services
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! 

If you're working with SAP Customer Experience (CX) solutions, you’ve probably heard about Continuous Quality Check (CQC) services. But what are they really about, and how can they help you? Let’s break it down in plain language.

What Are CQC Services?

Think of CQC services as a health check for your SAP systems. They’re designed to make sure everything is running smoothly and to catch potential issues before they become big problems. It’s like giving your car a tune-up before a long road trip—better safe than sorry, right?

For SAP CX solutions, there are two key CQC services available:

  1. CQC for Business Process Performance Optimization (BPPO): This service is all about making things faster and smoother. It looks at the way your critical business processes run within SAP Sales and Service Cloud and offers advice on how to speed things up and avoid bottlenecks. If your team has ever felt frustrated with slow response times or hiccups in workflows, this service is a game-changer. Note: This service is only available for SAP Sales and Service Cloud.

  2. CQC for Deployment Readiness: Whether you’re just about to go live with a new system or adding new features, this service ensures you’re set up for success. It checks your system’s configuration and analyzes things like security, performance, and stability. This service supports a wide range of SAP CX solutions—from SAP Sales and Service Cloud to SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Customer Data Cloud, and SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement. It’s like having an expert double-check everything before you flip the switch.

Why Should You Care?

Implementing or optimizing SAP systems can be a complex process, and nobody wants surprises during go-live or day-to-day operations. These CQC services give you peace of mind by catching potential issues early and providing actionable recommendations.

Here’s what you’ll get:

  • A detailed analysis of your system.

  • Practical tips to fix issues and improve performance.

  • Recommendations aligned with SAP’s best practices.

When to Use These Services

Not sure when to book a CQC? Here’s a quick guide:

  • CQC for BPPO: Use this when your system’s performance isn’t meeting expectations or when you’re handling complex processes that require fine-tuning.

  • CQC for Deployment Readiness: Book this before going live with a new implementation, adding new features, or as a health check for existing systems.

How to Get Started

Requesting a CQC service is simple. Just log in to the SAP for Me portal and select “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services” under the support options. Pro tip: SAP recommends booking the service about six weeks before your go-live date to ensure there’s enough time to make adjustments.

For more details, check out the information sheets for CQC for BPPO and CQC for Deployment Readiness to see how each service can help you.

Final Thoughts

At the end of the day, SAP’s CQC services for CX solutions are about making your life easier. Whether it’s optimizing performance or ensuring a smooth deployment, these services help you stay ahead of the game. So, why not give your SAP systems the care they deserve?

Have questions? Reach out to the SAP Customer Experience Value Map team or sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps to explore additional resources.

entry.title: Map time-dependant navigation attribute to transaction data 0calmonth in BW4
entry.content:
BW3Hello BW community

I have a problem to map time-dependent navigation attributes to transactional data. I would like to drill down with year / month (0CALMONTH) on the rows, and I need to get for each month the corresponding attributes which are valid for the month from master data. Currently, the system gets the recent values from master data. I tried to use a key data in the query according to the value entered from user, but it works for a specific date but not for all months. 

For Master data, I am using both ADSO and tried to join it to transaction data coming from acdoca in Composite provider, or I can use an info object with time dependent navigation attributes and join it with ADSO transactional data in composite provider too.

please advise

entry.title: VIM / OpenText Function Modules link/documentation
entry.content:
HI Everyone,

Where can I find the documentation of Function Modules Created by VIM in what website should I look? I cannot find the correct site

and question, who created the FM "/OPT/*****" is it SAP or OpenText or the SAP customers developers ? I am not yet familiar on this kind of scenario and objects. My expectation is SAP created this because the name of FM starts with "/" and did not start from "Z"

entry.title: Report: GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary has the values visible for Amount Awaiting Receipt
entry.content:
While running the GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary report, values are showing for Amount Awaiting Receipt, while that particular purchasing document line item is fully delivered and invoiced.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Report: GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary has the v...
entry.content:
You checked that particular purchasing document ID where the affected line items are fully delivered and invoiced, the report GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary is still showing the value in the field Amount Awaiting Receipt,

One of the possible reason for this might be that for the relevant Purchase order, GR/IR balance summary report showing both the amount awaiting receipt and the quantity waiting receipt because clearing set was adding to purchasing line item, usually it should not consider the clearing set in such scenarios.

This clearing set addition might be determined in the invoice creation process. So basically to avoid this issue you have to cancel the follow up supplier invoice and recreate again. Now try to run the GR/IR Inventories - Balance Summary for that particular purchasing document, there wont be any value under the Amount Awaiting Receipt for the same purchasing document item.

entry.title: #COE#TAIT How to download data from Monitor value chain app
entry.content:
Hi,

Due to price difference, we have many intercompany supplier invoices with errors. Do you know how to download bulk data from monitor value chain app to quick identify related Intercompany SO and PO?

entry.title: Re: Problem when calling a method from an include program
entry.content:
Make sure that you call the method from the same client and same system. Ask a fellow developer to look at it, it's obviously something you didn't pay attention to (classic issue which happens to everybody).

Also, you're talking about SY-SUBRC instead of looking at ULTIMA_FECHA, so maybe you're looking at SY-SUBRC after the method call in your test program (but not in your program with includes), which is always reset to 0 "when executing ENDMETHOD" (see the ABAP documentation concerning method calls).

entry.title: Re: Deleting multiple records - SAP MDK
entry.content: In that case you need to check your odata model and possibly modify it to support Cascade Deletes if that is an option or you have to first delete the dependent items before you can delete this one.

entry.title: Re: Not recommended feature: Session control statement is used in Dynamic SQL
entry.content:
Hi @virendra_raval, i've tried to execute the sql (alter system....etc) but appears an error message:

Could not execute 'alter system alter configuration ('indexserver.ini', 'system') set ('sqlscript', ...' Error: (dberror) [258]: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid 'CF3872CAFA31CD43A7C47CCAC7B149B8'.

Do you know any solution for this error?

entry.title: User access restriction is not working for Open and close periods
entry.content:
You have set up the user's access rights with no write access, only read access to work center view Sets of Books and Assigned Companies. Nevertheless, the user is still able to make changes to Open and Close Periods.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: User access restriction is not working for Open and c...
entry.content:
You have restricted write access for FIN_CLOSINGCOCKPITGL (Closing Cockpit) and FIN_COMPANIES (Sets of books and assigned companies) for the relevant business role but still the Open and close periods is can be accessed/edited by the user.

Navigate to the certain business role and go to the assigned work center view, there you see FIA_CLOSINGCOCKPITAUDIT Workcenter ID has read and write access for required Company.

If you set the read and write access for the company it will allow the user to set process control for that company and it will also allow the user to open and close the period for that company.

That is why you can see that the user is able to open and close the period. You have to restrict this workcenter id similar to how you did for FIN_CLOSINGCOCKPITGL and FIN_COMPANIES workcenter and then try to check if the user is able to open and close the period by saving any open and close period. it will throw an error that "you are not authorized to change the data".

entry.title: Re: Not recommended feature: Session control statement is used in Dynamic SQL
entry.content: The message is: Could not execute 'alter system alter configuration ('indexserver.ini', 'system') set ('sqlscript', ...' Error: (dberror) [258]: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid 'CF3872CAFA31CD43A7C47CCAC7B149B8'. Do you know any solution for this error?

entry.title: Re: OData service for Fiori Analytical Table - sap.ui.tab...
entry.content:
I think that can be a starting point:
Analytics Annotations 

You might also check out https://github.com/gregorwolf/sap-support-message-reporting and transfer that to ABAP.

entry.title: Automatic selection of bank is not getting picked.
entry.content:
Is there any option to avoid liquidity checks in a bank? Suppose I want to pay where liquidity allocation is not required.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Automatic selection of bank is not getting picked.
entry.content: Basically the bank account selection in the payments is also determined by the Liquidity Status, Minimum Amount, Overdraft Limit of the bank and this cannot be avoided as it default requirement to maintain.

entry.title: Miro document should not create more than po value
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have issue 

Microsoft document value should not exceed po value. If it exceed it should stop or pop up error message.

I tried with m8-082 error 

Gr-ir is unable checked in po, as per business requirements.

entry.title: Service procurement end to end Integration with SAP Fieldglass and SAP S/4HANA(22K)
entry.content:
SAP’s S/4HANA and Fieldglass solutions offer a comprehensive approach to streamline the end-to-end process(via scope item 22K)—from job postings to payments for contingent, SOW and SOW workers. Let’s dive into the key process flow, business benefits, and standout features that make this integration a game-changer.

Key Process Flow

  1. Create Purchase Requisitions: Generate purchase requisitions in SAP S/4HANA for each job posting and statement of work after creating a work order.
  2. Approve Purchase Requisitions: (Optional) Approve requisitions directly in SAP S/4HANA.
  3. Create Purchase Orders: Convert approved requisitions into purchase orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  4. Approve Purchase Orders: (Optional) Approve purchase orders within SAP S/4HANA.
  5. Submit Time for Approval: External workers log their time in SAP Fieldglass, which is then submitted for managerial approval.
  6. Create Service Entry Sheets: Generate service entry sheets in SAP S/4HANA. Approval is optional but can be enabled with minor configuration tweaks.
  7. Propose Invoices: Suppliers receive invoice proposals based on submitted time data in SAP Fieldglass.
  8. Post Invoices: After supplier approval, invoices are posted in SAP S/4HANA.

 

Business Benefits

Integrating SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fieldglass delivers numerous advantages, including:

  • Timely and Automated Payments: Reduce the cost and risk of outstanding payments with automated processes.
  • Accurate and Compliant Booking: Ensure precise recording of time and expenses for the external workforce.
  • Enhanced Productivity: Streamline workflows with integrated purchase requisitions, purchase orders, service entry sheets, and supplier invoices.
  • Improved Data Consistency: Achieve seamless data replication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Fieldglass for reliable system-wide consistency.

Key Features of the End-to-End Process

1. Faster and Accurate Close with Self-Billing

Self-billing, also known as Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS), automates the settlement of goods and service receipts. With ERS, suppliers agree not to submit invoices for purchase order transactions. Instead, the system automatically posts invoice documents based on purchase order and goods receipt data, eliminating invoice variances.

 

Benefits of Self-Billing:

  • Automatic settlement of service entry sheets
  • Prevention of invoice variances
  • Faster transaction closure
  • Elimination of non-value-added work
  • Centralized tax management

SAP S/4HANA’s scope item 22K supports this feature, allowing selective activation via supplier master data (look for ERS checkbox on supplier master data in SAP S/4HANA which will be replicated to SAP Fieldglass automatically via MDI).

2. Automatic Accruals Management

SAP S/4HANA integrates accruals management for unapproved time and expenses recorded in SAP Fieldglass. This feature ensures compliance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and enables timely cost and revenue recognition.

 

Value Proposition:

  • Recognize costs and revenue without delays
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards

By leveraging SAP’s integrated solutions, organizations can streamline workforce procurement, improve accuracy, and drive operational excellence. Whether it’s automating self-billing or managing accruals, the possibilities are transformative. Ready to explore these capabilities?

entry.title: Re: Passing values in Input Parameters - Variables of Datasphere Model from Power BI
entry.content:
Hello Jinal,

This is great workaround, but could you please suggest if we can do something for Import data connection option?

Thanks,

Ankur

entry.title: Re: Problem when calling a method from an include program
entry.content:
Sandra:

Thanks for your post. I’m quite sure that I’m using the same client (have my clients coded by color, BTW).

About using a fellow developer have a look at the problem, will try to do so. Unfortunately, I haven’t been able to get them to use those new constructs (Methods) 🙂 .

I checked both, SY_SUBRC and the data value stepping thru the method (debug). What I don’t get is why when calling the method from the program with includes (which, unfortunately was written by another developer, in order to muddle the things a little bit more) I get different results.

BTW, I added a comment to your recent post about using AI to document a program. If you have the time check it out.

Thanks again.

entry.title: Re: Passing data from one session to another session
entry.content: depending on the use case, shared objects may also be an option. if shared memory is not meant by this.

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion: Link Tiles not language sensitive?
entry.content:
Hi Carlo,

The objects in the Help Topics tab of SAP Companion, i.e. message bubbles and link tiles, are all stored in one content object in Manager for any given application screen.  So, if I log into S/4HANA or any other application running SAP Companion and then open a screen and start creating link tiles, all of those link tiles are added to one content object in Manager.

If the application screen language, at the time that you create the EN and DE link tiles, is English both link tiles will end up in the English object and so both will be shown when you open the application in English.  Language is considered but it is not the language of the individual link tiles that is considered but the language of the object that contains in the link tiles in Manager.

So, if you want to do what you mention you must:

1. Log into the application, e.g. S/4HANA, in English
2. Navigate to the relevant application screen and create the English link tile
3. Change the application language to DE
4. Create the DE version of the link tile

Now, the correct link tile will be visible based on the language of the application user interface that the user is using.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Connect to MSSQL Database from SAP BTP CAPM application
entry.content:
A few things: 

1. There should not be a problem connecting to MS SQL server if you have the right node package. 

2. Your problem seems to be a problem that it cannot find or resolve the host name of your server. 

3. There is at present no "standard SAP SQL Server adapter". For node, the only ones that are available (for Node) are HANA, Postgres and sqlite. 

4. If you wanted to build it correctly, you would package your SQL adapter in a CAP plugin "shell" similar to the adapters you find in https://github.com/cap-js/cds-dbs

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create a CRUD SAP Fiori Elements ap...
entry.content: I am talking about a backend handler. Here is a step-by-step we put together a couple of years ago: https://github.com/RizInno/cds-customer-stepbystep/

entry.title: Default profit center is not derived for the foreign currency remeasurement's journal entry.
entry.content:
Your company has default profit center for cash subledger in business configuration settings. When you run foreign currency measurement run, there is no profit center in that journal entry.

Now you have to create manual journal entry so you can move that to profit center. You want to know is there possibility like how you can use default profit centers for foreign currency measurement runs?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Default profit center is not derived for the foreign...
entry.content:
Whether a Profit Center is derived for Foreign Currency Remeasurement (Cash) depends on applied Reporting Principle.

For example if the Reporting Principle assigned to the set of books which is Funct of exp, profit ctr/seg [Profit center and segment]. This implies that for certain bank has no Profit Center is assigned for ongoing transactions.

You can illustrate this e.g. in Report ‘Journal’ for relevant Bank Account. Consequently no Profit Center is derived from resulting foreign currency remeasurements, regardless of Default Profit Center Definitions.

The situation would be different for assigned Reporting Principle "Funct of exp, profit ctr/seg, balancing" or "Funct of exp, profit ctr/seg, clearing".

With identical settings transactions on this bank account (e.g. bank-transfers) would debit/credit the Bank G/L Account and a Profit Center would be assigned – which in turn  would be assigned in Foreign Currency Remeasurement for Cash.

For additional information recommend to consult the Help-Document: "Reporting Principles"

Assigning Reporting Principles to Sets of Books

Profit center and segment [2]

With postings to income statement accounts, this reporting principle derives the profit center, and from the profit center it derives the segment.

In addition, the profit center is derived for postings to some balance sheet accounts (for example, material accounts). This makes it easier later to switch to a reporting principle that creates balance sheets by profit center.

In summary: Profit Center Postings against bank g/l account or EXR Gain/Loss account in this scenario will happen in case the applied Reporting Principle is "Funct of exp, profit ctr/seg, balancing" or "Funct of exp, profit ctr/seg, clearing".

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects Private Cloud Edition and SAP Ana...
entry.content: According to the current PAM (January 2025), BI 4.3 has mainstream maintenance until 12/31/2026. BI 2025, which will be generally available on March 12, 2025, has mainstream maintenance until 12/31/2027. The Statement of Direction has been updated with a successor, BI 2027, which has mainstream maintenance until 12/31/2029.

We have a requirement where we are moving from SAP ECC to S/4HANA Public cloud. As part of this we wanted to understand, how easy and flexible is the integration of OpenText VIM with S/4HANA Public cloud? What version is compatible? Currently we use VIM on the Sales Invoice and Scheduling agreement side.

Can someone please confirm how seamless the OpenText Integration is and should we be using CPI or BTP to integrate or connect OpenText with S/4HANA Public cloud?

Thanks,
Velu

entry.content:
Hi @mmitro  i was getting the same error as you.

Try adding the dependency for the sap.uxap library in the following files:

in the ui5.yaml

In the manifest.json

It worked for me !!

entry.title: Navigating SAP's AI Ecosystem: A Strategic Guide for Finance and Procurement Leaders
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, artificial intelligence has moved beyond being a technological novelty to become a critical driver of operational excellence and competitive advantage. For finance, procurement, and accounting leaders, understanding how to leverage SAP's comprehensive AI portfolio effectively can be the key to unlocking unprecedented efficiency and insights within their organizations.

 

Understanding SAP's AI Portfolio

SAP has structured its AI offerings into three distinct categories, each designed to address different organizational needs and readiness levels:

Base AI represents the foundation of SAP's intelligent enterprise vision. These embedded machine learning features come included with your existing subscription, offering immediate value without additional investment. These capabilities are already woven into your day-to-day operations, enhancing processes from automated invoice matching to predictive analytics for cash flow management.

Premium AI elevates your capabilities with advanced generative AI solutions. These sophisticated tools require AI units—SAP's measurement of computing power consumption—and can be seamlessly integrated into your existing workflows. Think of AI units as the fuel that powers these advanced features, with consumption varying based on the complexity and scale of your use cases across SAP's solution portfolio.

Joule, SAP's AI agent, represents the newest frontier in enterprise AI assistance. Available with a free subscription up to certain usage thresholds, Joule serves as your intelligent business companion, capable of handling complex queries and automating sophisticated workflows across your financial operations.

 

Strategic Implementation: A Practical Approach

To maximize the value of SAP's AI capabilities while ensuring responsible resource allocation, we recommend following a structured approach to implementation:

First and foremost, begin with a clear business need. The most successful AI implementations enhance already robust processes rather than attempting to fix broken ones. Take time to articulate specific challenges within your financial or procurement operations where AI could drive meaningful improvements.

Next, explore SAP's extensive catalog of AI features through the SAP Discovery Center Business AI Features portal (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/?category=pp.5). This comprehensive resource allows you to match your business needs with existing capabilities, potentially saving significant time and resources in custom development.

Once you've identified promising features, engage with your Customer Success Manager or Account Executive. These professionals can provide detailed insights into implementation requirements, AI unit needs for Premium features, and ROI calculations tailored to your use case. To better understand the resource requirements, utilize the AI Feature Estimator (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-estimator) for accurate projections of AI unit consumption and associated costs.

When existing features don't precisely match your needs, consider these alternatives:

1. Joule Integration: Explore how SAP's AI agent can be deployed to address your specific use case, taking advantage of the free subscription tier to pilot the solution.

2. Custom Development: The SAP Business Technology Platform offers a robust foundation for building customized AI solutions. Access the full catalog of AI services through the BTP AI services catalog (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/servicessearch/AI%20Enabled) and explore existing AI missions (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missionCatalog/) that might align with your objectives.

3. Partner Solutions: The SAP Store (https://www.sap.com/store.html) showcases AI solutions developed by certified partners, offering pre-built solutions that might address your needs without requiring custom development.

 

Making the Right Choice

Success in implementing AI within your financial operations depends on careful evaluation of your needs, resources, and objectives. Consider the following factors:

Implementation Timeline: Base AI features offer immediate value with minimal setup, while Premium AI solutions might require more extensive preparation but deliver more sophisticated capabilities.

Resource Requirements: Factor in both the initial investment and ongoing AI unit consumption for Premium AI features. The AI Feature Estimator provides essential insights for accurate cost projections and resource planning.

ROI Potential: Work with your SAP representatives to develop detailed ROI calculations for each potential implementation, ensuring your AI investments align with business objectives.

 

Next Steps

Begin your AI journey by:

1. Documenting specific challenges within your financial or procurement processes

2. Reviewing available features in the SAP Discovery Center

3. Scheduling a consultation with your SAP representative to discuss potential implementations

Remember, the goal is not to implement AI for its own sake but to drive meaningful improvements in your operations. Take advantage of demonstration sessions and pilot programs to ensure each implementation delivers tangible value to your organization.

 

For more information about specific features or to begin your AI implementation journey, visit the SAP Discovery Center's AI catalog or contact your SAP representative. The resources linked throughout this article provide comprehensive guidance for each stage of your AI implementation journey.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to tell if a Learning Item has been included in a Program?

entry.title: Exciting Job Opportunity for an SAP Professional!
entry.content:
We are looking for a skilled Sr. Solution Analyst, SuccessFactors with EC Payroll and preferably SAP ECC Payroll experience to join our team.

At Nutrien you can experience…

  • Innovative Projects: Be part of cutting-edge initiatives that implement large-scale transformation projects that push the boundaries of SAP technology.
  • Personal Growth: We believe in nurturing the whole person, from offering pathways to career advancement to assisting your wellness journey.
  • Collaborative Culture: Join a company that values knowledge-sharing, innovation, and teamwork. You would work in an inclusive workplace where everyone feels safe, has a sense of belonging, trusts one another, and acts with integrity. 

 

If you’re looking for a new opportunity, I encourage you to explore the position we have available. Your knowledge, skills, and experience could be a perfect fit for our team. Select the link for your preferred location.

  • Remote US - https://jobs.nutrien.com/job-invite/20575/
  • Remote Canada - https://jobs.nutrien.com/job-invite/20574/
  • US Colorado - https://jobs.nutrien.com/job-invite/20324/
  • Canada Saskatoon - https://jobs.nutrien.com/job-invite/20323/
  • Canada Calgary - https://jobs.nutrien.com/job-invite/20224/

Feel free to reach out to Devarati Roy (devarati.roy@nutrien.com) for more details or to apply directly via our website. We’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Business rule in Employee Central to check the start date of an absence under administer time
entry.content: Employee central: I want to write a business rule that checks the start date of the absence record created under the employee's profile - administer time UI, and if the start date is before a specific start date, then an error should be displayed on save.  I checked the sap blogs and see that we need to write a Take Rule, but not sure where to assign this take rule?  Can anyone guide me on this?

entry.title: Re: How to add a level in Concur via BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FI...
entry.content:
Hi, will take the chance and add more information about BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL.

First a couple of official documentations that can be found about BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL:

- Topic "7.1.2.2 Customer-Specific Hierarchy Level for Concur List" in SAP Concur Integration Setup Guide: SAP Integration with Concur Solutions - Setup Guide.

- Topic "I want to add a custom level to the org structure" in PDF file attached to KBA Comprehensive Guide on Utilizing Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) for SAP Concur Integration with Examples.

In summary BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL can be used to fill custom cost object hierarchy levels that are not covered in standard SAP Concur Integration. The SAP standard code fill out data for the following objects: Business Area, Company Code, Controlling Area, Country, Logical System, Cost Object Type and Cost Object ID.

All listed objects above are already filled by ICS and no BADI is required, but in case you need a Profit Center in-between the hierarchy like the image below, this level must be filled through BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL.

The List Level / Hierarchy Level is available at CTE_SETUP > Click on the relevant cost object integration, for example Cost Center > Change export > step Cost Object Hierarchy.

The Profit Center field/level above is not available in the standard drop down menu, and it must be created at option "Manage Custom Fields" (button available on same screen) where you are able to assign an ID and Value to the Custom Field:

Click on + to add an extra line to input the ID and Value, and - to remove not desired lines.  Also, option "Show BAdI Implementation" navigate to BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL screen.

You can add more fields / levels if required, for example a level for Employee Personnel Number:

Confirm/Enter at "Manage Custom Fields" to make available the new field at Field Name drop-down:

Once the additional custom fields are created, and hierarchy structure is configured as desired, for example:

Complete the wizard to confirm the changes, and implement BADI_CTE_FIN_COBJ_FILL_LEVEL creating different implementations for each custom field, assigning as Filter Value the Custom Field ID created at "Manage Custom Fields".

For Z_BADI_FILL_EE_LEVEL the filter:

And for Z_BADI_FILL_PC_LEVEL:

According to the above, during export of cost objects, system will call each BADI implementation separately where the Profit Center and/or Employee levels are used.

As soon as implementations are active, the column "BAdI Implemented" is true for each field in "Manage Custom Fields". You can double click the line and it will navigate to the relevant BADI implementation.

SAP ERP integration with SAP Concur solutions
 SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Concur solutions

entry.title: API Key - unable to load
entry.content:
Under my settings, I can see I am clearly logged into the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

However, I get the below error message.

"Please try again later" is a lazy way to inform users for any application. If there is a problem, it will not go away if you try 1 hour later or 1 month later!

So, the question is who can be contacted to fix the issue.

entry.title: Question about PPF
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I'm writing a PCE Customer Onboarding Form (COF).

The customer is trying to use the SAP PCE Private IPv4 Subnet (/22) as 100.9.0.0/22 and I want to know if it's OK to use it in the PCE Primary Data Cennter IP range.

For your information, this IP does not overlap with the customer's IP.

Regards,

YE

entry.title: Re: Issue with i18n Configuration in SAP CAP: Only Translates to the Base Language
entry.content: I've been able to test a different language in the past pretty easily using the Locale Switcher Chrome add-on. I'd recommend doing something like that to test out your application in another language.

entry.title: how to stop running sql on database
entry.content:
Hello,


We are running a clean up script for the upcoming release which will process 5 million records.


In case we need to stop the script in mid-execution or terminate the process, would that be possible? 


Thank you


PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: how to stop running sql on database
entry.content: use sql SHOW FULL PROCESSLIST; to show all of the process, then use KILL <PROCESS_ID>;
